Solving CORS in Nuxt 3 with Nitro Proxy
A quick note on how to use Nitro Proxy to solve CORS issues in Nuxt 3.
Nuxt 3 uses a brand new server engine called
, which ships with a Proxy feature that makes it easy to deal with CORS during dev.
I hit a small gotcha while playing with Nitro Proxy recently, so I figured I'd write it down.
Per Nitro's official example, you'd write:
language-ts shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast
Nitro's Proxy is built on top of
Say your remote API looks like:
language-bash shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast
This does solve CORS, but you'll hit another issue — no matter what you do, requests go to:
Weird, right? So I checked the Proxy Request and found that Nitro Proxy rewrites
to empty. You need to write it like this instead:
Now requests hit the right URL.
Nitro Proxy is designed to solve CORS during development, so don't use it in production. It's for frontend → API requests; the backend (SSR) won't hit CORS in the first place.
, but the official docs still mark it as "
", so I haven't bothered with it.
If you'd rather use Vite's Proxy instead of Nitro's, the same example becomes:
Fixing CORS in Vite With Proxy
before. The config is basically the same, so you can grab it from there.
